sanskrit:
प्रजहाति यदा कामान्सर्वान्पार्थ मनोगतान्।
आत्मन्येवात्मना तुष्टः स्थितप्रज्ञस्तदोच्यते॥
transliteration: prajahāti yadā kāmān sarvān pārtha mano-gatān | ātmany evātmanā tuṣṭaḥ sthita-prajñas tadocyate ||
literal_meaning: When one gives up all desires of the mind, O Pārtha, and is content in the self by the self, then one is called steady in wisdom.
contextual_meaning: True psychological stability arises when the mind no longer chases internally generated cravings and finds intrinsic satisfaction in self-awareness alone.
psychological_state: Secure-autonomous: the mind has shifted from object-referral to self-referral, ending compulsive seeking.
trigger_scenario: You silence your phone, close all apps, and discover you can enjoy an evening without scrolling, shopping, or snacking—just being.
anchor_text: Find lasting peace by dropping mental cravings and resting content in simple self-awareness.
